name: Dumbbell Hip Thrust
form_cues: Upper back on bench, feet flat, shins vertical at lockout
DB on hip crease — use a pad or towel if it digs
Drive through heels; squeeze glutes hard at the top for 1 sec
notes: Heavier-load glute movement than the floor glute bridge. Larger ROM = more glute stretch + contraction. Tuck chin and ribcage to avoid hyperextending the low back at lockout.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back braced against a flat bench, feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart, knees bent. The bench should hit just below your shoulder blades.
Place a single dumbbell upright on your hip crease (use a hip pad or folded towel for comfort). Hold the dumbbell steady with both hands.

Drive through your heels and extend your hips so your torso forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Shins should be vertical at the top — adjust foot position if knees travel past or stop short.
Squeeze your glutes hard at lockout for a 1-second pause, then lower under control without resting the dumbbell on the floor between reps.
